title: Govern stage2_variable_sweep_and_table_compaction_v1 as the maintained S2-2 class-level repair
decision: Treat variable-sweep rescue, evidence-table compaction, duplicate suppression, and S2-2/S2-3 boundary validation as the governed maintained S2-2 repair set.
reason: The validated multi-paper repair set closes missing_variable_table, table_flattening, false_role_coverage, evidence_budget_overflow, and near_duplicate_evidence without sentinel regression.
impact: The maintained Stage2 composite path now records the class-level repair in the repair index, mem_v1, and maintained-script registry for future baseline runs.

title: Decision: Lock the summary-only coverage-first S2-2 / S2-4a selector contract
decision: Keep all S2-4a table evidence summary-only, reduce selector authority to conservative denoising plus minimum coverage plus bounded packing, classify tables only as must_include/optional_context/hard_drop, and return semantic table scoping authority to the LLM.
reason: Targeted replay improved or stabilized INMUTV7L, V99GKZEI, L3H2RS2H, and QLYKLPKT without reintroducing full-table prompt surfaces or coarse-label semantic veto.
impact: Governed Stage2 docs, audit checklist, repair index, and maintained script surfaces must treat selector semantics as coverage-first and summary-only while keeping semantic table judgment LLM-owned.

title: S2-2b confirmed-noise-only table preservation
decision: Irreversible S2-2b table removal is limited to confirmed pure noise; if a table is not confirmed noise, preserve it in the pre-LLM authority surface.
reason: 5ZXYABSU showed that formulation-bearing Table 1 and Table 2 survived S2-2a but were wrongly hard-dropped at S2-2b, creating irreversible summary-only loss before the LLM.
impact: Future selector work must reject importance-based table suppression and treat audit/debug labels as observability rather than preservation vetoes.

title: S2-3/S2-4a summary neutrality and structure-first summary contract
decision: The maintained S2-3/S2-4a summary path is neutral across preserved tables; the main residual risk is lossy compression, and header schema plus first-column row identity are the primary summary contract while sample rows are optional aids only.
reason: Prompt-path audits showed no normal-path primary-table promotion across preserved tables, but structural compression can still hide formulation detail.
impact: Future summary work should preserve schema and row-identity structure rather than reintroducing table-importance ranking or sample-row dependence.

title: Authority reopen metadata is deterministic execution-side metadata
decision: authority_run_dir, authority_payload_root, and related reopen handles are deterministic execution-side metadata and must not be treated as LLM semantic content or depend on the LLM to transmit them.
reason: Replay from frozen raw responses stayed semantically reusable but failed when authority metadata was missing from replayed semantic docs, proving the need for deterministic sidecar/reattachment handling.
impact: Replay and downstream deterministic execution must recover authority handles through governed sidecars or reattachment surfaces rather than semantic-doc dependence.

title: Prefer replay from frozen raw responses when the LLM-facing contract is unchanged
decision: When LLM task, prompt content, evidence content, model, and generation settings are unchanged, downstream deterministic contract changes should prefer replay from frozen raw responses over fresh live calls if execution-side metadata can be lawfully reattached.
reason: Recent Stage2 repairs were entirely downstream of frozen S2-4b raw responses, so replay reduced nondeterminism and kept validation scoped to the changed contract.
impact: Future deterministic Stage2 repairs should default to replay-first validation and use fresh live calls only when the LLM-facing contract changes or replay cannot lawfully reattach metadata.

title: Bounded simple formulation-table deterministic enumeration after semantic authorization
decision: Stage2 may deterministically enumerate base rows for low-ambiguity non-DOE full_formulation tables after LLM table authorization using preserved normalized_table_payload authority; this path does not require LLM row-level output and does not apply to DOE, sweep recovery, or cross-table decode cases.
reason: INMUTV7L preserved a simple numbered formulation table upstream, but the frozen raw response kept only a family-level semantic object. Bounded S2-7 replay validation recovered 12 deterministic rows from preserved authority without changing prompt text or requiring new live calls.
impact: Simple formulation tables can now be recovered deterministically after semantic authorization while WIVUCMYG remains on DOE, 5GIF3D8W remains on non-DOE single-variable recovery, and UFXX9WXE remains stable without regression.

title: Decision: Restore UFXX9WXE DOE target binding with file-derived table IDs and tolerant numbered-row detection
decision: Bounded replay validation restored UFXX9WXE deterministic DOE enumeration to 26 rows by preferring file-derived __table_N__ identity over conflicting caption-derived numbers and by tolerating spaced numbered labels like 7 . during numbered-row detection.
reason: Current live baseline recognized DOE scope but failed to bind Table 10 back to preserved authority because S2-2 recovered the authority asset under the wrong table ID and lost numbered-row structure on the companion table.
impact: UFXX9WXE DOE execution now resumes on governed replay; WIVUCMYG and YGA8VQKU remain stable in the same bounded validation.

title: Decision: Recover corrupted split-column variable-sweep tables with source-backed row recovery
decision: Use bounded source-backed row recovery in table_row_expansion_v1 for LLM-authorized variable-sweep tables whose preserved payload stays repair_insufficient/unrepaired_corrupted.
reason: L3H2RS2H retained lawful rowwise concentration evidence in preserved source CSV and source-text table blocks even though standard direct-row extraction emitted zero rows.
impact: Bounded and full replay restored L3H2RS2H from 7 to 21 final rows without collateral count drift on other papers.

title: Decision: Collapse later measurement-only rows that reuse DOE labels onto existing DOE formulation cores
decision: For DOE papers, later non-DOE measurement/post-processing rows that reuse a unique deterministic DOE formulation label and only add before/after processing characterization variables must collapse onto the existing DOE formulation core rather than create a new formulation.
impact: Stage5 duplicate-governance now collapses these later labeled measurement rows; WIVUCMYG moved from 29/26 to 26/26 and no other paper counts changed in the full compare collateral run.

title: Decision: Recover anchorless sequential-optimization single-variable rows from stagewise source-text contracts when row-level raw replay collapsed to family summaries
decision: When a replayed sequential-optimization paper preserves stagewise optimization semantics and selected-condition hints but no longer materializes row-level candidates, Stage2 may recover synthesis-defining single-variable rows directly from explicit stagewise source-text level lists, provided downstream/post-processing variable axes are excluded and an explicit anchor scope is not already available elsewhere in the document.
impact: Bounded replay restored QLYKLPKT to 7 final rows and full replay improved the compare surface from 12/15 to 13/15 matches with no collateral count drift after guardrailing against papers with existing explicit anchor scopes.

title: 5ZXYABSU shared-parameter evidence from user paper check
decision: For 5ZXYABSU, PVA is the stabilizer; Polysorbate 80 and Labrafil are added surfactants, with Polysorbate 80 in the external phase and Labrafil in the inner phase.
reason: User-provided source text describes base nanoprecipitation in 1% PVA, NPR2 using 0.5% PVA + 0.5% Polysorbate 80 external phase, and NPR3 using 3.5 mg Labrafil in the inner phase.
impact: Layer3 GT and compare normalization should not treat PVA as the surfactant on the modified rows, and should preserve paper-local surfactant vs stabilizer roles.

title: 5GIF3D8W method/solvent split evidence from user paper check
decision: For 5GIF3D8W, PLGA rows use nanoprecipitation with acetone, while PCL rows use emulsion solvent evaporation with dichloromethane; the methods do not use a mixed solvent together.
reason: User-provided source text states nanoparticles were prepared by nanoprecipitation and emulsion solvent evaporation using PLGA polymers and PCL, respectively; acetone was used for PLGA and dichloromethane for PCL.
impact: Layer3 GT and compare normalization should split method_type and solvent_name by PLGA vs PCL row identity rather than carrying a combined shared value across both families.

title: YGA8VQKU shared-parameter evidence from user paper check
decision: For YGA8VQKU, acetone is explicitly reported as the solvent and Poloxamer 188 is explicitly reported as the aqueous-phase surfactant/stabilizer factor (cP188), but method_type is not explicitly classified into a governed canonical method label and LA/GA is not explicitly reported per formulation row.
reason: User-provided source text shows PLGA 75:25 polymers of two viscosities in materials, solvent displacement technique in methods, acetone as organic solvent, aqueous Poloxamer 188 solution as surfactant solution, and cP188 as a concentration factor in the experimental design.
impact: Layer3 GT/compare should treat acetone and Poloxamer 188 as explicit reported values, treat cP188 as a paper-local alias for Poloxamer 188, and avoid overstating method_type or row-level polymer_grade/LA:GA when the paper does not explicitly report them per formulation.

title: WIVUCMYG polymer-grade/shared-parameter evidence from user paper check
decision: For WIVUCMYG, Resomer® 753S is explicitly stated in the materials as the shared PLGA material across the paper, while the production method, solvent, and PVA shared parameters are also explicitly reported in the methods.
reason: User-provided source text states PLGA Resomer® 753S, PVA aqueous phase, acetone organic phase, and solvent displacement technique in the preparation section.
impact: Layer3 compare should treat the remaining mismatch class in WIVUCMYG primarily as a polymer-grade surface/carry-through issue rather than a shared-parameter binding ambiguity.

title: INMUTV7L shared-field and label semantics from user paper check
decision: For INMUTV7L, solvent displacement method and acetone are explicitly reported as shared preparation fields, while the formulation labels encode row-specific polymer/surfactant combinations such as '7 PLGA 10% PVA' meaning formulation 7 with PLGA-PEG 10% and PVA. The paper text also treats PVA, Tween 80®, and Lutrol F68 as surfactants.
reason: User-provided source text states solvent displacement method, acetone, 90 mg polymer in 5 mL acetone into 10 mL aqueous surfactant solution at pH 3.5, and explicitly explains the optimized concentrations of PVA, Tween 80®, and Lutrol F68 plus the formulation-label interpretation before Table 1.
impact: Layer3 GT/compare should use solvent displacement method as the explicit method_type surface, acetone as shared solvent, and row-label semantics to recover polymer/surfactant assignment on numbered formulations; the remaining no-unique-alignment row for '7 PLGA 10% PVA' is a compare/alignment issue rather than a paper-boundary ambiguity.

title: UFXX9WXE Layer3 source-text decode
decision: UFXX9WXE source text explicitly states PLGA 50:50 molecular weight 30,000–60,000; preparation method is emulsion solvent evaporation (nanoprecipitation) with acetone organic solvent and poloxamer 407 aqueous surfactant; Table 1 defines X1 polymer concentration 10/35/60 w/v, X2 surfactant concentration 2/8.50/15 w/v, X3 aqueous/organic phase ratio 2/6/10 v/v, X4 drug concentration 1/3/5 w/v; Table 2 decodes these as PLGA mg/mL, Poloxamer mg/mL, w/o phase volume ratio, and Drug conc. mg/mL; optimized formulation is X1 10 mg/mL, X2 9.42 mg/mL, X3 10, X4 4.5 mg/mL with experimental z-average 168.2 nm, PDI 0.08, entrapment 83.8%, and drug loading 8.7%.

title: Decision: Value authority and typed direct-value contract for Layer3 fields
decision: Row-local table-cell bindings are highest numeric value authority; row-local table assignments with typed headers are next; shared carrythrough must be source-backed and scoped; LLM semantics authorize scope but not numeric type; compare/source-CSV rebinding is diagnostic/fallback only. Direct benchmark values must not silently include derived %w/v×volume or ratio×mass calculations.
reason: Diagnostic 115 reduced risk rows 1812 to 1806, improved drug_mass_mg recall 0.395349 to 0.627907, improved polymer_mass_mg canonicalized accuracy 0.500000 to 0.685714, and removed polymer_mass_mg extra values 7 to 0 while lowering polymer_mass_mg recall due to rejecting invalid mass values.
impact: Use typed validators rather than value blocklists; mass fields reject identity, ratio, concentration, and volume tokens.

title: layer3_mass_carrythrough_derived
decision: Compare 123 added guarded shared preparation mass carrythrough and separate derived_mass_provenance_json. It improved polymer_mass_mg by 4 matches vs 115 (5GIF3D8W 50 mg rows), kept drug_mass_mg 115 gains, emitted zero derived rows, and left ACTIVE_RUN unchanged because diagnostic-only collateral remains.

title: Promote polymer_mass_mg scope-guard diagnostic baseline
decision: ACTIVE_RUN points to compare 130 as diagnostic baseline after scoped shared preparation polymer mass and ordinal_grid_semantics guard.
reason: General field contract reduced polymer_mass_mg pollution without paper-specific hard-fill; residual blocked_alignment/derived cases remain explicitly skipped.
impact: Layer3 error queue 1812->1799 vs compare 98; polymer_mass_mg extra 7->0 and canonical accuracy 0.5->1.0; recall lower because polluted nonempty values were removed.

title: S2-4a structural-only prompt freeze refresh
decision: S2-4a prompt rendering must reject stale cached table-summary text unless it already has summary_contract: structural_prompt_view_only and column_schema and has no table_role_hint, semantic_summary, or key_columns.
reason: Old frozen prompt artifacts can carry pre-LLM semantic labels in cached table summaries even after source-table rehydration is repaired; the generic guard prevents stale prompt surfaces from being re-promoted.
impact: Current refreshed artifact has 15/15 prompts, 92/92 structural table summaries, 0 forbidden semantic markers; diagnostic-only, not benchmark-valid final output.

title: p9 generic Stage2 authority reattachment and sweep guard repair
decision: Record p9 as a cross-paper generic Stage2 repair: clean-text authority reattachment for malformed table payloads remains deterministic S2-5 recovery, and S2-7 single-variable expansion must be source-observed endpoint/baseline gated; compare remains diagnostic-only.
reason: Lineage 429->434 from current working tree closes target L3H2RS2H/QLYKLPKT/5ZXYABSU/7ZS858NS and 5GIF3D8W, keeps QLYKLPKT 7/7 not over-counted, and has no count regression vs current 428 diagnosis baseline.
impact: Eligible only for diagnosis-baseline pointer consideration, not benchmark-valid promotion.

title: Decision: Skip publisher chrome when compacting S2-2 table summary sample rows
decision: Compact TABLE_SUMMARY sample rows should continue scanning until non-noise source/table rows are found, rather than taking the first two data rows unconditionally.
reason: 5ZXYABSU and QLYKLPKT cleantext/table readthrough surfaces can place publisher chrome, title/author spillover, references, or submission footers at the top of extracted CSVs; using those as sample rows creates S2-2/S2-4a boundary noise while full payload authority remains valid.
impact: S2-2 DEV15 replay 438 and S2-4a/hard-gate replay 439 pass for 5ZXYABSU and QLYKLPKT. This is diagnostic-only and does not claim benchmark-valid improvement.

title: Decision: Use context_inheritance_markers as Stage2-to-Stage3 handshake
decision: LLM emits selection_markers and context_inheritance_markers as semantic relation cues with field_value allowed for source-backed shared fields; Stage3 materializes relation rows and Stage5 consumes explicit resolved fields only.
reason: QLYKLPKT had correct 7-row closure but shared preparation fields did not legally propagate without an explicit marker contract.
impact: Bounded QLYKLPKT diagnostic 481->482->484 keeps 7 final rows and fills shared fields 7/7; full DEV15 fresh live validation is next.

title: Decision: Do not promote fresh DEV15 context-marker route
decision: Leave ACTIVE_RUN.json unchanged after fresh DEV15 context-marker route.
reason: Although QLYKLPKT shared-field propagation works and count is 7/7, full DEV15 final rows drop to 140 vs 202 GT rows with four under-counted papers.
impact: Context-marker handshake remains bounded validated repair; full-route promotion is blocked pending row-discovery/count repair for L3H2RS2H, UFXX9WXE, YGA8VQKU, and RHMJWZX8.

title: Stage5 source-group direct-field retention guard accepted
decision: Promote the Stage5 source-group direct-field retention guard as current diagnostic baseline while keeping broad Stage3 table-cell-grid measurement binding diagnostic-gated.
reason: Collapsed final groups can choose a representative row with blank direct fields even when a sibling source row in the same final group carries a unique row-local table-cell direct value; retaining only unique within-group direct values fixes WIVUCMYG PDI without cross-row inference.
impact: DEV15 Layer1 remains 202/202 and 15/15 match; Layer3 present_and_match improves 1836 to 1862 and missing_in_system improves 606 to 580 with no mismatch, extra, or blocked regression vs run 593.

title: Dictionary-backed table-cell-grid measurement binding accepted
decision: Enable Stage3 table-cell-grid measurement binding by default using canonical_field_for_header, unit/shape guards, row identity matching, and Stage5 paper-scoped resolved-field lookup.
reason: The earlier broad binding false positives were caused by two generic issues: Stage5 resolved relation lookup was keyed only by formulation_id, allowing F1 collisions across papers, and Stage3 fallback header matching allowed non-percent Drug content (µg/mg) into percent fields. Both are now fixed generically.
impact: DEV15 Layer1 remains 202/202 and 15/15. Layer3 run 609 vs prior active 601 improves present_and_match 1862 to 1865, present_but_mismatch 553 to 550, extra_in_system 110 to 106, with missing_in_system 580 and blocked_alignment 420 unchanged.

title: S2 metric-tail measurement guard accepted
decision: Stage2 S2-7 compatibility projection now reads DOE/result metric-tail field=value strings from change_descriptions and may overwrite only suspect PDF glyph/cid table-cell measurement values; Stage3 measurement binding rejects cid pseudo-values and implausible measurement ranges.
reason: WFDTQ4VX DOE rows had correct particle size values in recovered change_descriptions but final table consumed polluted (cid:4)1 table-cell values as size_nm=4. The same cid pseudo-values also entered Stage3 measurement binding, so the fix needed both S2 consumption and S3 value-quality guards.
impact: DEV15 Layer1 remains 202/202 and 15/15. Layer3 run 614 vs prior active 609 improves present_and_match 1865 to 1870 and present_but_mismatch 550 to 545, with missing_in_system 580, blocked_alignment 420, and extra_in_system 106 unchanged.

title: Decision: Add material MW registry v2 and row-local concentration grid consumption
decision: Stage5 materialization now uses dictionary-backed molecular-weight cues plus a Da/kDa range parser to populate source-backed polymer_mw_kDa surfaces, and consumes Stage2 table_cell_grid row-local concentration bindings into polymer/drug concentration value and unit fields.
reason: Current run614 Material missing analysis showed polymer_mw_raw/polymer_mw_kDa and UFXX9WXE concentration-unit cells remained blank despite source text and S2 table-cell-grid evidence already carrying the necessary authority.
impact: Full DEV15 diagnostic replay 615->617 preserved Layer1 202/202 and 15/15; Layer3 present_and_match improved 1870->2020 and missing_in_system reduced 580->430 with no present_and_match regression.

title: Accept EE numeric-covariate mainline repair
decision: Accepted the three-step plan: optional S2 typed relation_cues, Stage5 concentration value/unit and DOE coded/physical guards, then Stage5 metric-tail measurement binding for EE/size covariates.
reason: This keeps LLM formulation identification primary while adding typed optional cues and deterministic materialization guards; validation improves Layer3 matches without Layer1 regression.
impact: ACTIVE_RUN advanced to Stage5 692, Layer1 compare 693, and Layer3 compare 694 as diagnosis-only baseline.

title: Accept Stage5 entity-role identity-variable materializer
decision: Stage5 may materialize row-local identity variable/value pairs into final typed bundles only when the role is explicit or the article-local entity is grounded by existing row/source evidence.
reason: This preserves LLM row membership authority and avoids giving generic rules semantic row-creation power while fixing compare-surface gaps such as etoposide amount -> drug_feed_amount_text.
impact: ACTIVE_RUN advanced to Stage5 718, Layer1 compare 719, and Layer3 compare 720 as diagnosis-only baseline.

title: Accept S5-2c deterministic numeric value/unit structuring
decision: Stage5 may split already-materialized physical concentration surfaces into final numeric value and unit bundles while preserving the original combined text surface; Layer3 compare may canonically compare surfactant/stabilizer percent concentration unit surfaces.
reason: This preserves final-output structure for future numeric modeling without row creation, unit conversion, GT backfill, or S5-3 LLM backfill; run726 improves present_and_match 2572->2616, missing_in_system 434->395, and present_but_mismatch 168->163 vs run720.
impact: ACTIVE_RUN advanced to Stage5 724, Layer1 compare 725, and Layer3 compare 726 as diagnosis-only baseline.

title: Accept generic DOE factor physical-value materializer
decision: Stage5 may decode row-local DOE coded levels into final physical value/unit bundles only when the source defines a factor/level map; compare must not treat unresolved coded combined surfaces such as -1 mg/mL as physical concentration fallback.
reason: This replaces pH/paper-specific behavior with a bounded generic materialization rule while preserving LLM/S2 row authority and avoiding unit conversion, GT backfill, row creation, or S5-3 LLM numeric backfill; run729 improves present_and_match 2616->2678, missing_in_system 395->385, and present_but_mismatch 163->111 vs run726.
impact: ACTIVE_RUN advanced to Stage5 727, Layer1 compare 728, and Layer3 compare 729 as diagnosis-only baseline.

title: Accept generic DOE table continuation and actual-value materialization
decision: S2-7 may recover split table factor definitions by selecting coded-level header rows and merging non-value continuation text; Stage5 may materialize row-local Xn_actual physical assignments into final value/unit bundles without re-decoding.
reason: This addresses table flattening misalignment and checkpoint/actual DOE values generically while preserving active S2-7/Stage3 row authority; run737 improves present_and_match 2678->2711 and missing_in_system 385->352 vs run729 with no mismatch, extra, or not_reported regression.
impact: ACTIVE_RUN advanced to Stage5 735, Layer1 compare 736, and Layer3 compare 737 as diagnosis-only baseline.

title: Accept guarded generic table structure profile
decision: S2-2/S2-7 may emit a structural table_structure_profile describing orientation, header regions, data regions, and column-record hints, but profile records must not authorize or instantiate primary formulation rows.
reason: This creates a generic table-geometry sidecar for non-DOE transposed matrices while preserving LLM semantic row authorization; the follow-on hardening makes the profile hint-only in S2-7 so table geometry cannot redefine the row universe.
impact: ACTIVE_RUN advanced to Stage5 746, Layer1 compare 747, and Layer3 compare 748 as diagnosis-only baseline after the hint-only boundary replay; Layer1 remains 202/202 and 15/15, and Layer3 is unchanged vs run743.

title: Adopt multi-evidence-anchor selector boundary
decision: S2-2 selector must preserve multiple distinct source-body evidence anchors across categories and must not stop after one category slot is filled.
reason: Evidence multiplicity is semantic input for the LLM, not a deterministic category quota; large-campaign audit found hard-gate-passing papers where stronger source-body anchors remained in candidate_blocks but were omitted from S2-4a evidence.
impact: Adds bounded multi-anchor preservation before live LLM calls; no row creation, no formulation membership decision, no GT backfill, no semantic redundancy decision, and no deterministic variant collapse.

title: Filter empty context inheritance marker schema shells before S2-6
decision: S2-5 parser drops context_inheritance_markers that have no source label, table id, targets, inherited fields, held fixed conditions, or evidence cue.
reason: The LLM can emit an all-empty schema placeholder for an absent marker; that object is not semantic discovery and should not block the strict S2-6 marker validator.
impact: Full campaign S2-6 changed from one ZB76MB3J contract error to error_count=0 across 376 documents while preserving real partial and execution-ready context markers.

title: Guarded source-text DOE payload fallback inside LLM-authorized S2-7 scope
decision: S2-7 may materialize explicit numbered clean-text table rows only when the LLM has declared a DOE table row enumeration scope; fallback rows must have positive, unique, contiguous labels and may beat lossy Marker payloads when they expose more rows.
reason: UFXX9WXE and 7WLX2UBI had LLM DOE authorization but current campaign normalized payloads were missing or row-label lossy; an initial unguarded replay regressed 6AT9RFVD by treating coded 0 factor levels as formulation rows.
impact: Guarded replay 120->121->122 produced 1042 final rows vs child115 1011 with no negative paper-level final deltas; positive deltas were UFXX9WXE +23 and 7WLX2UBI +8.

title: Decision: do not promote 196 executable-scope path as GPT35 row-count repair
decision: Executable-scope composition/preparation hints may be preserved as adapter signals, but the 196 raw responses lack enough row-universe semantics to recover 139 DEV15 behavior downstream.
reason: Bounded replay changed 5GIF3D8W S2-7 skip reason from missing_llm_variable_roles to insufficient_column_header_rows, while S2-7 and Stage5 counts stayed unchanged.
impact: Future GPT35 repair should preserve the 139-compatible row-materialization semantic shape and add GPT35 fields as auxiliary signals rather than replacing the contract with executable-scope authorization.

title: Decision: reject broad 139+ GPT35 auxiliary prompt after DEV15 gate failure
decision: The first true 139+ prompt attempt preserved the 139 top-level schema but added a broad GPT35 auxiliary signal block; it must not be promoted because DEV15 five-paper gate regressed.
reason: Stage5 counts changed from 139 baseline 5GIF3D8W 26, 5ZXYABSU 9, L3H2RS2H 21, UFXX9WXE 27, WFDTQ4VX 30 to 139+ R1 counts 6, 9, 14, 26, 30.
impact: Future GPT35 repairs should avoid broad live prompt wording changes; use narrower feature-gated prompt deltas or deterministic downstream repairs from already-present 139-style raw signals.

title: R2A narrow 139+ row-universe prompt rejected
decision: Do not promote the R2A narrow row-universe prompt branch to GPT35 because the five-paper DEV15 gate still regressed versus 139.
reason: Although R2A improved 5GIF3D8W versus broad R1, it left 5GIF3D8W at 20 versus 139/GT 26 and L3H2RS2H at 14 versus 139/GT 21; WFDTQ4VX also dropped at S2-7 from 60 to 33 before Stage5.
impact: Future GPT35 repair should not rely on global prompt wording changes until deterministic replay/materializer options are exhausted; R2A remains diagnostic-only negative evidence.

title: R2B deterministic replay from existing 168 signals selected as positive diagnostic candidate
decision: Prefer R2B as the next GPT35/EE35 repair base over new prompt variants because it improves row recovery without any new live LLM calls.
reason: Replaying 170 through current deterministic S2-7/Stage3/Stage5 increased S2-7 rows 259->285 and Stage5 final rows 212->233 versus 184/186, with 3 improved papers and 0 paper-level final-row regressions in the count audit.
impact: Next repair should analyze remaining gaps from the R2B audit, especially JRMKHP5C, XDIRIJ74, 2RNHC2M5, YZYKTTFE, and KTNLRQZU, before considering more S2-4a prompt edits.

title: R2B-R3 primary NP sample-ID guard accepted as positive diagnostic candidate
decision: Treat nanoparticle sample IDs such as HbNPs-5 as primary formulation identity labels so LLM-authorized partial formulation table rows are not filtered as measurement-only non-primary rows.
reason: JRMKHP5C Table 1 rows were recoverable from authority rows but non_primary_direct_rows_look_measurement_only filtered HbNPs-style labels because formulation_identity_label_looks_primary did not recognize them as primary IDs.
impact: Full EE35 no-live replay improved S2-7 285->296 and Stage5 233->244, with JRMKHP5C 21->32 and 0 paper-level count regressions; diagnostic-only pending identity-level GT alignment.

title: R2B-R4 rowwise sample-ID label canonicalization
decision: Preserve native primary first-cell sample IDs during rowwise formulation materialization instead of replacing them with synthetic row_XX labels.
reason: JRMKHP5C Table 3 rows were present but carried synthetic labels such as row_01__hbnps-7, weakening GT identity alignment and downstream row joining.
impact: R2B-R4 kept S2-7 and Stage5 counts unchanged at 296 and 244, reduced JRMKHP5C naive missing GT labels 24->18, and had 0 paper-level count regressions versus R2B-R3.

title: R2B-R5 guarded packed source-row sample-ID recovery
decision: Recover multiple prefixed NP sample IDs from a single authorized source CSV line, but apply cross-table duplicate suppression only to newly augmented packed_prefixed_sample_id_source_row rows.
reason: JRMKHP5C Tables 1 and 2 stored multiple real HbNPs-* identities in one flattened source row; a first dedup attempt regressed ZB76MB3J by filtering legal repeated condition rows, so the guard had to be narrowed.
impact: R2B-R5 guarded increased EE35 S2-7 rows 296->314 and Stage5 rows 244->262 with 0 paper-level count regressions versus R4. JRMKHP5C improved 32->50 and missing GT labels 18->2, but remains diagnostic-only due to +5 over-retention residual.

title: DeepSeek S2-4b initial trial configuration
decision: Use frozen S2-4a prompts; start S2-4b DeepSeek trial with deepseek-v4-flash, JSON Output enabled, thinking disabled, no cache settings; reserve pro/thinking-high for bounded comparisons.
reason: Flash likely sufficient for bounded semantic JSON task but needs validation; JSON Output improves structure; provider cache is automatic.
impact: DeepSeek provider/model parameters affect S2-4b raw-response lineage only and do not alter S2-4a or S2-5+.

title: DOE decoded pH precedence and physical-value guard
decision: DOE factor assignments now record factor_value_kind and decoding_rule so coded design levels can be decoded while already-physical table values pass through without a second decode; Layer3 compare prefers final typed pH_raw bundles before coded DOE fallback.
reason: Active run654 showed 42 pH_raw mismatches where final Stage5 already held decoded physical pH values but compare returned raw coded DOE levels; user also identified the risk that later DOE result tables may already contain physical values despite a separate coding table.
impact: Run656 keeps Stage5 652 and Layer1 653 unchanged, improves Layer3 present_and_match 2403->2445 and present_but_mismatch 387->345 vs run654, and reduces pH_raw coded_doe_factor_rebinding mismatches 42->0 without changing row counts.

title: Generic shared-parameter relation bundle
decision: Stage3 relation resolution may carry arbitrary source-backed shared field names; Stage5 preserves all resolved fields in shared_parameters_json and only projects governed core fields to typed benchmark columns.
reason: Per-field shared carrythrough rules do not scale to unknown paper-specific variables.
impact: Allows unbounded shared parameter transmission without widening the final table per variable; run145 -> run148 compare statuses unchanged.

title: Generic capability restoration for value materialization repairs
decision: Missing direct-value repairs must restore generic capability rather than patching specific papers or isolated fields; concrete papers are validation cases only.
reason: Paper-specific abbreviations, strings, and one-off field rules will not generalize across heterogeneous PLGA articles.
impact: Future S3/S5-2 repairs should be framed around paper-local material alias graphs, entity-value binding, scope-aware shared carrythrough, canonical promotion, and field-type validation with authority guards.

title: Subtype-scoped surfactant concentration carrythrough
decision: Rows with exactly one nanosphere/nanocapsule subtype may inherit a unique explicit preparation surfactant/stabilizer concentration from that subtype scope; duplicate unit extras are suppressed when GT value already contains the same unit.
reason: ACTIVE_RUN 135 had residual emulsifier_stabilizer_concentration_value missing cells with safe direct evidence in subtype-specific preparation paragraphs.
impact: Run 138 improved value recall 0.574324->0.682432 and overall error_rows 1773->1757 with no mismatch/extra/blocked increase.

title: polymer_mass scoped preparation carrythrough accepted
decision: Use source-backed direct preparation mass carrythrough for explicit parenthetical material labels and unique nanocarrier subtype scopes; exclude mg/mL concentration mentions from direct mass.
reason: Run 130 low recall reflected safe carrythrough gaps after pollution cleanup, not GT correction need.
impact: polymer_mass_mg recall improved 0.333333 to 0.612903 with accuracy 1.000000 and no match regressions.

title: Protocol inheritance marker handshake for same-procedure overrides
decision: Stage2 should expose same-procedure/protocol override markers, S3 should materialize them as targeted candidate_protocol_override_field relation rows, and S5 should consume only resolved relation fields.
reason: 5ZXYABSU showed the source sentence "same procedure but incorporating 5 mg Gat" was previously outside the prompt evidence pack or not consumable downstream, leaving drug_mass_mg missing.
impact: Bounded diagnostic replay restored 5ZXYABSU NPG drug_mass_mg for 3/6 aligned GT rows without spreading 5 mg Gat to NPR/NPB rows; no ACTIVE_RUN update until full DEV15 validation.

title: Protocol inheritance exact inherited fields for phase volumes
decision: Stage2 protocol_inheritance_markers should carry exact unchanged source-protocol values in inherited_fields, while overrides remain target-specific changes; S3 materializes inherited fields separately and S5 consumes them into typed final bundles.
reason: 5ZXYABSU showed external aqueous phase volume was semantically inherited from the same procedure but was not represented as a consumable exact field and could remain only in shared_parameters_json.
impact: Bounded 5ZXYABSU run 561 improved external_aqueous_phase_volume_mL from 0/6 to 6/6 present_and_match; full DEV15 run 570 improved Layer3 missing_in_system vs run 548 but Layer1 run 569 remains 192 vs 202 rows, so ACTIVE_RUN is not updated.

title: Generic role-header value-type guard
decision: Generic role headers such as surfactant/polymer/drug concentration may populate concentration values but must not create concrete material names, polymer mass, or drug feed amount fields; source-backed unique material identities from text remain eligible for shared carrythrough.
reason: WFDTQ4VX showed Poloxamer emitted from a generic surfactant concentration header even though the source text identifies Pluronic F68; the same pollution class could affect polymer and drug concentration headers by filling mass/feed slots.
impact: Run679 removes WFDT Poloxamer pollution, preserves UFXX source-backed Poloxamer 407, keeps Layer1 202/202 and 15/15, improves Layer3 present_and_match 2476->2549 and present_but_mismatch 314->186 vs run657, with pH match retained.

title: Role-tolerant emulsifier/stabilizer canonicalization
decision: Layer3 compare now passes the shared value-normalization lexicon into the role-tolerant surfactant/stabilizer union overlay and canonicalizes conservative surface variants such as PVA parentheticals, Tween80/Tween 80, Pluronic F-68/F68, and Solutol HS15.
reason: Active run656 retained 71 role_tolerant_union_overlay mismatches even when the difference was only surface spelling or parenthetical expansion because the merged overlay bypassed the lexicon and had thin name canonicalization.
impact: Run657 keeps Stage5 652 and Layer1 653 unchanged, improves Layer3 present_and_match 2445->2476 and present_but_mismatch 345->314 vs run656; role-tolerant bad cells fall 76->45 while missing, extra, and not_reported counts stay unchanged.

title: Layer3 Stage3 shared numeric relation fields
decision: Stage3 resolved_relation_fields already emitted shared numeric/text fields, but Stage5 consumed only a narrow text whitelist. Expanded Stage5 resolved-field consumption with typed guards; removed centrifugation_g/time from core compare surface; run140 diagnostic error_rows 1757->1638 with mismatch/extra unchanged.

title: Layer3 concentration direct identity and surfactant binding split diagnostic
decision: Advance active diagnostic baseline to run142 after direct row-identity drug concentration recovery and bounded emulsifier/stabilizer name-concentration value/unit split; GT authority TSV was not mutated.
reason: Run142 produced a positive diagnostic delta vs run140: error_rows 1638->1598, present_and_match 1635->1675, missing 779->741, mismatch 85->83, extra/blocked unchanged.
impact: Improves direct concentration and surfactant/stabilizer compare accounting while preserving remaining QLYKLPKT value conflicts and WFDTQ4VX/YGA8VQKU residuals for source/GT adjudication.

title: Run143 O_volume_mL row-local direct volume authority activated
decision: Run143 activated row-local direct O_volume_mL authority diagnostically.

title: Shared entity-bound preparation parameter carrythrough
decision: Stage5 may carry a unique direct preparation-context solvent volume into organic_phase_volume_mL_* when the row already has the bound organic_solvent identity; globally shared drug_name can authorize direct drug mass carrythrough for eligible loaded rows.
reason: Run143 residual O_volume_mL errors were mostly final-table materialization gaps: rows had organic_solvent but no O_volume field bundle.
impact: Run145 vs run143: error_rows 1585->1515; O_volume_mL present_and_match 20->90 and missing 104->34; 70 missing_in_system->present_and_match; mismatch/extra/blocked unchanged.

title: Guarded emulsifier/material registry diagnostic baseline
decision: Accept run163 as the next diagnostic baseline after bounded row-local surfactant identity, guarded concentration materialization, source-backed material registry recovery, and compare-surface guards.
reason: run154 had positive gains but introduced polymer_grade extras, drug_name false-positive risk, and surfactant concentration mismatches; run163 repairs those regressions.
impact: Layer3 error_rows 1456->1417 vs run150; present_and_match 1817->1835; missing 599->583; present_but_mismatch 83->81; extra 186->165; blocked_alignment unchanged 588; benchmark_valid remains no.

decision: Generic endpoint-aware ratio direction repair accepted diagnostically: parse ratio endpoint variables/order for all ratio fields (LA:GA, polymer:drug, drug:polymer, polymer:solvent, phase water/oil or organic/aqueous); reverse only inverse-declared directions; avoid bare compact-ratio fallback for conflicting/direction-neutral fields. run166 error_rows 1607->1603 vs run165; benchmark_valid=no.

title: Generic concentration factor materialization
decision: Diagnostic run172 added bounded generic source-defined concentration-factor materialization for row-local X/cMaterial assignments; run166->run172 error_rows 1603->1559, missing 836->780; benchmark_valid=no.

title: Generic row-local formulation identity drug-name rebinding
decision: Accept bounded compare-surface extraction of drug_name from row-local formulation_identity_label/table_row_id when direct drug field is blank, rejecting blank/control and ambiguous labels.
reason: L3H2RS2H residual audit showed direct row labels carried XAN/3-MeOXAN while system drug_name surface was blank.
impact: 16 L3H2RS2H drug_name cells missing_in_system->present_and_match; error_rows 1559->1543; no mismatch/extra/blocked/non-target changes.

title: Row-code table scope recovery and generic material alias binding
decision: S2-7 may infer a formulation-table scope from LLM row-coded formulation candidates or protocol marker targets when table_scopes are missing, using preserved normalized payloads and bounded source_table_files; S3 protocol marker target binding should use generic paper-local material alias tokens rather than hardcoded article material names.
reason: 5ZXYABSU showed LLM semantic authorization through NPG/NPR row codes but no table_scopes, causing S2-7 to skip full Table 1 recovery; earlier S3 material binding used narrow Gat/Rh/polysorbate/Labrafil token logic.
impact: Diagnostic lineage 576->580 restores 5ZXYABSU Stage5 count to 9/9 and reduces external_aqueous_phase_volume_mL missing versus run 570; full DEV15 remains non-promotable because L3H2RS2H is +1.

title: Stage2 table expansion preserves semantic scope role lookup through authority aliases
decision: Use semantic/original scope id fallback for table_variable_roles after authority payload reopen, and reject prose-spillover optimization sentences as row-level authority assignments.
reason: QLYKLPKT replay exposed that reopened table payload IDs can differ from the LLM semantic table scope, causing role lookup to miss; source-text spillover can also overcount an optimization sentence as a variable row.
impact: Bounded diagnostic replay restored QLYKLPKT final count to 7/7 while preserving LLM-first semantic authority and downstream Stage5 filtering of non-synthesis variants.

title: Secondary source identity recovery and characterization-helper binding
decision: S2-7 may supplement existing LLM-authorized formulation scopes with preserved source-text/table-authority identity scopes that are not already active formulation tables; Stage5 should bind characterization-only LLM helpers back to matching table rows when drug, nanoparticle family, and concentration match.
reason: L3H2RS2H had main Table 1/3 enumeration but skipped Table 2 empty nanospheres, Table 4 empty nanocapsules, and Table 5 sample identities; after recovery, two LLM characterization helpers duplicated table rows.
impact: Full DEV15 diagnostic lineage 583->589 restores Layer1 to 202/202 and 15/15 match; Layer3 run 589 has present_and_match=1824 and missing_in_system=618. ACTIVE_RUN updated as diagnostic baseline; benchmark_valid remains no.

title: S2-1b high-confidence source denoise projection
decision: Add S2-1b between S2-1 scope resolution and S2-2 evidence construction; it emits denoised_for_stage2 text while preserving raw/current clean text as audit authority.
reason: DEV15 full-cleantext diagnostics showed obvious publisher/reference/header/footer noise entering candidate/evidence selection; existing cleanup was too late and too weak.
impact: Full original-source extraction should wait for no-live S2-1b->S2-2 acceptance; S2-1b must not perform formulation semantic discovery, table-importance selection, row-universe construction, or GT/source-anchor completion.

title: S2-4b explicit model boundary
decision: S2-4b live calls must receive an explicit --model; maintained code has no repository-wide default model identity.
reason: Model switching should affect only raw-response persistence at S2-4b, not S2-4a prompts, S2-5+ parsing/validation/projection, or unrelated live-call utilities.
impact: Changing --model creates a new S2-4b raw-response lineage; benchmark interpretation still requires aligned Stage2 completion lineage and GT authority.

title: S2 DOE factor schema completeness for coded assignments
decision: S2 numbered DOE recovery now emits structured row-local factor assignments with factor_token, factor_name, coded_factor_value, decoded_factor_value, factor_unit, source_table_id, and coding_table_id; Stage3 prefers structured factor labels/tokens before source-text fallback role maps.
reason: WFDTQ4VX had X1/X2/X3 design rows plus a companion factor-level table, but current Stage2 rows lacked complete row assignments and forced compare-side paper-specific coded overrides.
impact: Bounded lineage 623->629 proves WFDT structured assignment production and Stage3 typed fields, but is not promoted because Layer1 regresses to 207/202 from L3H2RS2H +5 retained LLM summary/characterization rows.

title: S5-3 is residual source-evidenced gap filling, not database completion
decision: S5-3 must not assume every article reports every target field and must not target blank schema/final-table slots merely because they are empty.
reason: PLGA literature reporting is heterogeneous; absent values may be true non-reporting rather than extraction failure, and dense database-completion would create false positives.
impact: S5-3 scope must be gated by benchmark/GT missing_in_system evidence or audited source evidence; not_reported/unresolved/no-source blanks are not extraction targets; mismatches and blocked alignment need separate workflows.

title: Stage1 unified source selection is HTML-first when duplicate current bindings exist
decision: For the unified current+Marker Stage1 handoff, prefer HTML current clean text/structure over duplicate PDF current bindings; use PDF as fallback and attach frozen Marker only for PDF rows where already available.
reason: The user original design was HTML-priority; duplicate key2txt/key2structure bindings had allowed PDF to win for many PDF+HTML rows, so source selection must be based on the selected binding path rather than manifest-level text_source_type defaults.
impact: Updated build_stage1_unified_current_marker_v1.py, maintained_script_surface, and src_script_registry; generated run-scoped unified_full_html_priority artifacts plus refreshed Stage2 admission/preview audit surfaces. No Stage2/LLM/ACTIVE_RUN update.

title: DOE factor assignment relation chain
decision: S2 numbered DOE recovery exposes row-local factor assignments; S3 emits candidate_doe_factor_field rows from structured assignments or legacy DOE change_descriptions; S5 consumes resolved doe_factor_assignment fields into canonical bundles with guards against negative coded concentrations.
reason: UFXX9WXE recovered all DOE rows but phase_ratio_raw stayed missing because w/o phase volume ratio remained a text-like row factor rather than a typed relation field.
impact: Full DEV15 diagnostic lineage 618->621 keeps Layer1 202/202 and 15/15; Layer3 present_and_match improves 2020->2046 and missing_in_system improves 430->404 vs run617, with mismatch/blocked/extra unchanged.

title: Measurement-table binding for characterization result fields
decision: Stage3 may emit candidate_measurement_binding_field relation rows when a preserved characterization/result table column binds to an already-authorized formulation identity; Stage5 consumes only resolved relation fields and writes size_nm, pdi, and zeta_mV as instance-specific typed bundles.
reason: L3H2RS2H preserved Table 2 and Table 4 characterization surfaces had size/PDI/zeta values for existing formulation rows, but the values were not bound back to final formulation identities.
impact: Full DEV15 diagnostic lineage 590->593 keeps Layer1 at 202/202 and 15/15 match; Layer3 present_and_match improves 1824->1836 and missing_in_system improves 618->606 with mismatch/extra/blocked unchanged.

title: Stage5 partial sweep summary retention guard
decision: Stage5 partial compact-sweep survival now requires explicit result-bearing evidence before an LLM summary/helper row can bypass complete-table-enumeration and parent-linked non-synthesis descendant filters.
reason: The S2 DOE factor schema diagnostic retained five L3H2RS2H LLM family/characterization summaries with no independent result evidence because the partial-sweep survival guard fired too early.
impact: Lineage 630->632 restores Layer1 to 202/202 and filters F1-F5; active-lineage replay 633->634 is byte-identical to active Stage5 run 619, but no ACTIVE_RUN update because DOE schema lineage Layer3 remains below active 621.

title: Stage5 guarded phase-volume carrythrough
decision: Stage5 may materialize blank organic_phase_volume_mL and external_aqueous_phase_volume_mL bundles from unique clean-source preparation-context phase-volume evidence, including solvent-volume statements without unique solvent identity, PVA/surfactant external phase volumes with concentration text, and carrier-scoped nanosphere/nanocapsule external aqueous volumes.
reason: Active run640 retained phase-volume missing fields even when source text contained preparation statements such as shared solvent volume, 12 mL of 1% PVA, or nanocapsule-specific 20 mL aqueous Pluronic solution.
impact: Guarded full DEV15 lineage 644->646 keeps Layer1 202/202 and 15/15; Layer3 present_and_match improves 2094->2108 and missing_in_system improves 356->342 versus run640 with mismatch, blocked, extra, and not_reported unchanged; ACTIVE_RUN advanced to 644/645/646 as diagnosis-only baseline.

title: Stage5 source-backed protocol/process carrythrough
decision: Stage5 may materialize blank global preparation process bundles for stirring_time_h, evaporation_time_h, and pH_raw only from unique clean-source preparation-context evidence, with guards against release/HPLC/buffer contexts and broad evaporation-under-stirring false bindings.
reason: Active run621 retained protocol/process missing fields even when direct preparation statements were present in clean source text because Stage5 global preparation carrythrough covered phase volumes and concentration fields but not process time or preparation pH.
impact: Guarded full DEV15 lineage 638->640 keeps Layer1 202/202 and 15/15; Layer3 present_and_match improves 2046->2094 and missing_in_system improves 404->356 with mismatch, blocked, extra, and not_reported unchanged; ACTIVE_RUN advanced to 638/639/640 as diagnosis-only baseline.
